workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Working on Wide World Importers's migration architecture. Got the escalation email -- help me assess the risk.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 16:04 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Wide World Importers

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 14:07 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 12:24 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers migration architecture

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-H trap (Skips prior-escalation pattern check at this account); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-H failure mode (Skips prior-escalation pattern check at this account)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-H -- Skips prior-escalation pattern check at this account
